chapter_title.ar: باب ضَمَانِ مَا يُصِيبُ الدَّوَابُّ وَ مَا لَا ضَمَانَ فِيهِ مِنْ ذَلِكَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 42 – Responsibility of what hits the animals and what is not a responsibility regarding it from that

text: قَضَى أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) فِي صَاحِبِ الدَّابَّةِ أَنَّهُ يَضْمَنُ فِي مَا وَطِئَتْ بِيَدِهَا وَ رِجْلِهَا وَ مَا نَفَحَتْ بِرِجْلِهَا فَلَا ضَمَانَ عَلَيْهِ إِلَّا أَنْ يَضْرِبَهَا إِنْسَانٌ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Fazzal, from Yunus Bin Yaqoub, from Abu Maryam,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> judged regarding the owner of the animal that he is responsible regarding whatever it tramples by its hands (front legs), and its (hind) legs; and whatever it kicks with its (hind) legs, so there is no responsibility upon him except if a human being strikes it’.
